item.title: ICI turns over flood control investigation documents to DOJ
item.description: The Independent Commission for Infrastructure turned over on Monday to the Department of Justice the results of its investigation into anomalies related to flood control projects, as the body winds down its operations.

item.title: Claire Castro seeks P500 million in damages from Leviste for ‘ruining’ her integrity
item.description: Communications Undersecretary Claire Castro on Monday confirmed that she sought P500 million in damages from Batangas Rep. Leandro Leviste, saying she is “entitled” to that amount after the lawmaker caused her anxiety and “for ruining [her] integrity.”

item.title: NBI says won't allow agency to be used for 'fishing expedition' as '18 ex-Marines' face scrutiny
item.description: NBI director Melvin Matibag told ANC's "Headstart" that the testimony of the alleged former soldiers admitted in their affidavit that they had not personally negotiated with the personalities accused of receiving pay-offs and that there was only a "conduit" for the alleged transactions.

item.title: Healthcare official testifies QC’s P16-M hospital information system procured by Herbert Bautista ‘not fully functional’
item.description: A P16-million Hospital Information System (HIS) procured by the Quezon City government in 2019 was flagged as not fully functional and lacking interoperability, according to a testimony presented Monday before the Sandiganbayan First Division.

item.title: Trillanes files cyberlibel complaints vs 18 'ex-marines,' SMNI hosts
item.description: Former senator Antonio Trillanes IV filed cyberlibel complaints before the Department of Justice against the 18 “ex-marines” as well as SMNI hosts who supposedly made statements on suitcases of money allegedly intended for International Criminal Court personnel.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
